scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced the job through readiness, execution, and completion states only when part, container, inventory, and closure evidence were explicit.
Missing revision, label, and approved-source prerequisites kept the job blocked and produced a bounded exception note rather than an unsupported start or ship recommendation.
Engineering and supplier approval authority were rejected cleanly and the request was routed without widening the lane.
Conflicting part, container, and ASN evidence across ERP, MES, WMS, and label systems was treated as a traceability blocker and prevented unsupported progression.
A quality or containment concern moved the work into hold posture, preserved evidence, and kept final release authority with quality.
Completion was refused until pick, ship, reconciliation, and signoff evidence existed, keeping lifecycle state transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Supplier nonconformance and broker-buy pressure were escalated to supplier-quality, procurement, change-control, and quality owners without unsupported source approval.
Low-confidence handling correctly requested CSR context, authoritative record locations, and release maps rather than guessing.
Conflicting source and tenant policy signals were summarized and escalated instead of being merged into a false approval answer.
A mid-process sequencing or packout exception was packaged as an execution exception with explicit rework and release boundaries instead of improvised closure.
Tenant terminology was adapted without losing IATF, AIAG, GS1, Odette, and NHTSA baseline controls, lifecycle-state discipline, or explicit assumptions.

notes: All 11 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to automotive parts execution, revision control, container and label traceability, hold and release boundaries, and completion evidence.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the audit contract.
